article_meta.title: Business Climate change study that overestimated economic impact is pulled — but not before banks relied on it By Taylor Herzlich Published Dec

reasoning: The summary remains neutral and factual, avoids the article’s more charged phrasing, and attributes claims appropriately. Minor over-assertiveness appears in calling 23% the “correct figure,” but overall it does not inherit loaded framing.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs and descriptors; slight emphasis in “severely skewed” and “flawed study,” but still factual.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States 23% as the “correct figure,” which overstates certainty; otherwise claims are attributed.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: “Influential” and “flawed” are mildly interpretive; “correct figure” implies certainty.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming language beyond facts.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about motives or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Balanced presentation including authors’ continued view on substantial impacts.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Covers major elements: retraction, data issue, institutional use, NGFS, authors’ stance.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Tone matches the article; includes mitigating context from authors.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Avoids the article’s more loaded lines (“catastrophic” etc.); mostly measured.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Misses explicitly naming the separate economists’ critique as a perspective in sources_represented; otherwise includes key voices.
